sourceMovie.title: Close
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Close works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Prestige
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Close clicked for you, The Prestige (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Close clicked for you, The Perks of Being a Wallflower (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Close?
faq.answer: Try The Prestige, The Perks of Being a Wallflower, Moonlight—each shares DNA with Close while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Close?
faq.answer: Close (2022) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Close worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
